name: Fillet of Steelhead Bonne Femme
recipeInstructions:
This lordly French dish is prepared in a variety of ways, but basically it
is fish fillets served with two sauces and mushrooms in between. The
fillets from small salmon, walleye, lake trout, and channel cat are all
superb prepared in this fashion.
If the two sauces seem too time-consuming, just note that this dish is
excellent if only the wine sauce is used.
1. Place the fish head, etc., peppercorns, and shallots into a pot of water
and white wine. Bring to a boil, then simmer gently for 30 minutes. Strain
and set aside.
2. Arrange the fillets in a shallow glass or earthenware fireproof dish that
has been liberally buttered. Add the bouquet garni.
3. Pour in the reserved fish stock and poach in an oven preheated to 325°F
for 20 minutes.
4. Sauté the sliced mushrooms in 2 tablespoons of butter for 5 minutes,
coating and stirring a couple of times. Reserve.
5. Prepare the hollandaise and hold it by covering with a lid.
6. Make the wine sauce by melting 2 tablespoons of butter in a pan, then stir
in the flour and cook a few minutes. Turn off heat, pour in the liquor from
the poached fillets, then stir and thicken over the fire. Add the milk,
then stir until it bubbles. Season to taste.
7. To assemble: lay the fillets on a fireproof dish and cover with the wine
sauce. Dot the top with mushrooms. Ladle the hollandaise sauce over all
and glaze under the broiler.
8. Garnish with cucumber and lemon slices or with watercress.
Hollandaise Sauce:
1. In a heavy pot, whisk eggs until well blended, turning lemon-colored,
and starting to thicken over low heat.
2. Gradually add butter one piece at a time, whisking each piece until
absorbed by the eggs. Continue until all the butter is used up (about 2
minutes). The sauce should be thick.
3. If separation occurs, quickly add a teaspoon of cold milk or cream.
4. Whisk in about 1 more teaspoon of lemon juice, a pinch of salt, and optional
cayenne. Taste to ensure it is lemony enough.
